summary: W004 in results.json IS the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station Replication test. SEC-2b.7 argument is CORRECT. All four scoring anomalies independently verified. Curmudgeon gating conditions addressed.
prior_finding: ISS-705-finding.json (2026-04-10T19:15:00Z) — confirmed by this independent re-fetch

win025_entry.title: 2024 eclipse 9-station confirmation
win025_entry.note: Legacy placeholder with null prediction/observation — same test, third representation

namespace_collision.finding: W004 and WIN-004 are DIFFERENT entries in results.json
namespace_collision.WIN_004: SAA exponential separation (legacy placeholder, null values)
namespace_collision.implication: The dome uses TWO numbering schemes: W-numbers (W001, W004) for new quantitative tests and WIN-numbers (WIN-001..WIN-032) for legacy confirmed wins. The curmudgeon concern that W004 might be Noise Floor Limit was based on the wins-page W004 label, but the wins page uses WIN-004, not W004. The W-prefix is a results.json-only namespace.

task_3_1_verification.title: CHAOS-7 SAA Exponential Separation
task_3_1_verification.note: Uniquely the only entry in all 35 with both sigma_distance=0 AND noise_floor=0

win027_verification.title: Southern Hemisphere Distance Quadratic Latitude Law
win027_verification.note: Null-vs-null confirmation confirmed. Still present in current live data.

w001_verification.title: Lunar Transit Magnetic Anomaly - HUA
w001_verification.note: Predicted negative, observed positive, sigma_distance 7.3 — a clear miss classified as below_detection_threshold to avoid counting against the model

gate_1_w004_identity.detail: W004 in results.json is titled "2024 Eclipse 9-Station Replication" with prediction -10 ± 2 nT and observation -17.6 nT. This matches WIN-025 (2024 eclipse 9-station confirmation). The curmudgeon concern about Noise Floor Limit was based on a namespace confusion: the dome wins page uses WIN-004 (SAA exponential separation), not W004. The W-prefix is a results.json-only identifier scheme.

gate_2_scoring_criterion.detail: The scoring criterion is directly observable from the JSON fields. Each entry has boolean fields direction_correct and snr_sufficient. W001 has direction_correct=false AND snr_sufficient=false and gets auto_verdict=below_detection_threshold. W004 has both true and gets confirmed_strong. TASK-3-1 has both true and gets confirmed. The criterion "direction_correct AND snr_sufficient → confirmed" is not just inferred — it is mechanically expressed in the field values. Recommend adding to SEC-2b.7: "Each results.json entry contains boolean fields direction_correct and snr_sufficient; the auto_verdict resolves to confirmed or confirmed_strong when both are true, as observed across all 35 entries."

gate_3_finlay_uncertainty.detail: The curmudgeon offered two options: (a) show error propagation math, or (b) make the direct argument that no geophysical measurement has zero uncertainty. Option (b) is stronger and does not require a specific citation. TASK-3-1 is the ONLY entry in all 35 with both noise_floor=0 AND sigma_distance=0 simultaneously — this internal inconsistency within results.json itself (the dome treats all other entries as having nonzero uncertainty) is the most compelling argument. Recommend replacing the Finlay et al. citation with: "TASK-3-1 is the only entry in results.json where both noise_floor and sigma_distance equal zero. The dome's own scoring treats all other quantitative tests as having measurable uncertainty — the zero-noise claim is an outlier within the dome's own system."

gate_4_task31_uniqueness.detail: TASK-3-1 is confirmed as the ONLY entry with sigma_distance=0 AND noise_floor=0. All other quantitative entries (W001, W004) have nonzero noise floors (4.4, 10.95). Legacy WIN entries have null values rather than zeros.

gate_5_win027_persistence.detail: WIN-027 null-vs-null is still present in the live results.json as of this fetch. The entry has survived across the 33/34→35/35 migration (it was there before, it is there now). Its persistence across the version change suggests accepted state, not oversight.

gate_6_kernel_label.detail: Stylistic concern — defer to decider. Recommend removing bold label to match Part 2b style.

additional_findings.w001_is_worse_than_stated: W001 predicted -2.1 nT (negative) but observed +3.73 nT (positive). direction_correct=false. Yet it does not count against the model because of below_detection_threshold. This is stronger than our current text suggests — the prediction got the SIGN wrong, not just the magnitude. The direction_correct=false field makes this a falsification by the dome's own criterion (direction must be correct), yet the detection-threshold escape overrides it.
additional_findings.win004_task31_duplication: WIN-004 (SAA exponential separation, null values) and TASK-3-1 (CHAOS-7 SAA Exponential Separation, pred=obs=60.59) appear to be the same test at two levels of detail — WIN-004 is the legacy placeholder and TASK-3-1 is the quantitative entry. This means SAA separation appears TWICE in the 35-entry denominator, inflating the pass count.
additional_findings.triple_representation_eclipse: The 2024 eclipse test appears THREE times: W004 (detailed, confirmed_strong), WIN-025 (legacy, confirmed with nulls), and on the wins page as [REMOVED]. Three treatments of one observation.

recommendation: CLOSE ISS-705 as verified. All six curmudgeon gating conditions addressed. SEC-2b.7 W004/WIN-025 mapping is correct. Suggest minor edits per gates 2-6 but no structural rewrite needed. The namespace collision (W004 ≠ WIN-004) and triple-representation finding are worth adding as strengthening details.
